subject: Mathematics
title: Goldbach's Conjecture
description: A quick win: understand Goldbach's Conjecture.

hook.text: In 1742, Christian Goldbach wrote a letter with a bold claim about even numbers.

buildup.text: He said every even number greater than 2 can be written as the sum of two primes.

discovery.text: 4=2+2, 6=3+3, 8=3+5, 100=47+53. It works for every even number ever tested.

twist.text: Computers have verified it up to 4 quintillion. But verification isn't proof.

climax.text: After nearly 300 years, it remains unproven. It's the oldest open problem in math.

punchline.text: Every even number hides two primes. Nobody can prove it.

quiz.question: What does Goldbach's Conjecture claim?
options: Every even number greater than 2 is the sum of two primes
Every number has a prime factor
